name: Belorado from Briviesca — circular via Villalomez and Valle del Río Retorto — Eurovelo EV01
name_original: Belorado desde Briviesca — circular por Villalomez y Valle del Río Retorto — Eurovelo EV01
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Briviesca offer diverse landscapes in the northeast of Burgos, Spain. The city is situated in a valley formed by the Oca River, nestled between the San José and Los Pinos mountains, and opens onto the plain of La Bureba. This varied terrain provides options for cyclists, from routes along the Oca River to climbs in the Montes de Oca. The region features picturesque scenery, including sections of the Camino de Santiago – Via de Bayona. Best road cycling routes around Briviesca The most popular road cycling route is Alto de la Verga from Briviesca — circular via Santa Marina and La Bureba, a 34.0 miles (54.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through the region's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Belorado from Briviesca — circular via Villalomez and Valle del Río Retorto — Eurovelo EV01, a moderate 33.2 miles (53.4 km) path. This route offers scenic views as it traverses the Valle del Río Retorto and includes a section of the Eurovelo EV01. Local road cyclists also love the Roadbike loop from Briviesca, a 14.6 miles (23.5 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 57 minutes. Road cycling around Briviesca is defined by river valleys, plains, and mountain foothills, offering routes with varied elevation profiles. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 99 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Briviesca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Briviesca?
A: Briviesca offers a diverse landscape for road cyclists, situated in the Oca River valley between the San José and Los Pinos mountains, opening onto the La Bureba plain. This means you'll find a mix of river valleys, expansive plains, and mountain foothills, providing varied elevation profiles from gentle stretches to more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Briviesca?
A: Yes, Briviesca offers routes for various skill levels. For those looking for an easier ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Briviesca&lt;/a&gt; is a great option. It's an easy 23.5 km (14.6 miles) trail through the local countryside, typically completed in under an hour.

Q: What are some popular moderate road cycling routes in the Briviesca area?
A: Many road cyclists enjoy the moderate routes around Briviesca. Two popular choices include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209002857/alto-de-la-verga-from-briviesca-circular-via-santa-marina-and-la-bureba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Verga from Briviesca — circular via Santa Marina and La Bureba&lt;/a&gt;, a 54.7 km route with significant elevation, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208990648/belorado-from-briviesca-circular-via-villalomez-and-valle-del-rio-retorto-eurovelo-ev01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belorado from Briviesca — circular via Villalomez and Valle del Río Retorto — Eurovelo EV01&lt;/a&gt;, which is 53.4 km and includes a section of the Eurovelo EV01.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders near Briviesca?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Briviesca offers more difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40309506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;MonasterioDeSanSalvador – Frías Bridge loop from Briviesca&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 97.1 km (60.3 miles) route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What kind of natural features and viewpoints can I find along the cycling routes?
A: The region around Briviesca is rich in natural beauty. Cyclists can encounter impressive views from locations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5580127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Busto&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the scenery from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4294915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PeñaCamino&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4294908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cueto&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4036282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Pan Perdido&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see while road cycling in Briviesca?
A: Yes, Briviesca's routes can lead you past historical and cultural sites. The city itself boasts a historic quarter with a medieval layout, Plaza Mayor, and churches like the Collegiate Church of Santa María la Mayor. The Camino de Santiago – Via de Bayona also passes through Briviesca, offering a blend of scenic and spiritual journey.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Briviesca?
A: The road cycling routes around Briviesca are well-regarded by the komoot community. Over 99 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountain foothills, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Briviesca?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Briviesca are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209002857/alto-de-la-verga-from-briviesca-circular-via-santa-marina-and-la-bureba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Verga from Briviesca&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208990648/belorado-from-briviesca-circular-via-villalomez-and-valle-del-rio-retorto-eurovelo-ev01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belorado from Briviesca&lt;/a&gt;, both offering circular journeys.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Briviesca?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, Briviesca's location in northern Spain suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring colder conditions, especially in the higher elevations of the Montes de Oca.

Q: Are there any facilities like public fountains along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes may pass by facilities. For instance, the area includes a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4632109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Public fountain in Villalómez&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a convenient stop for cyclists needing to refill water bottles.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Briviesca?
A: Komoot currently features over a dozen road cycling routes around Briviesca, offering a wide selection for cyclists of all levels, from easy loops to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: Can I find routes that are part of larger cycling networks, like Eurovelo, in Briviesca?
A: Yes, some routes around Briviesca incorporate sections of larger cycling networks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208990648/belorado-from-briviesca-circular-via-villalomez-and-valle-del-rio-retorto-eurovelo-ev01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belorado from Briviesca — circular via Villalomez and Valle del Río Retorto&lt;/a&gt; route includes a segment of the Eurovelo EV01.
tips: Considered the gateway to Burgos, the town of Belorado is located next to the Tirón River and with the passage of the Jacobean route and the crossing of EuroVelo 1 and 3 it is a very important link.

Before reaching the town of Tosantos we can find the valley that bears the name of the same river, Río Retorto. From this road you can see the formations created by the passage of water through the alluvial lands of this area.
Beautiful road that exists in this area of Valle de Oca and where in some of its curves, you can observe spectacular panoramic views.
Place where you can fill up with water whenever you need it and where, in addition, the town has impressive views of the Montes de Oca.

waypoints.name: Main Square of Belorado and Bridge over the Tirón River
